releaseNotes: v0.32.0 — four customer-facing improvements from the 2026-05-27 deferred queue. (1) A-Sync-2: drug name + dose now cross-device-sync within the same sync cycle as the bottle that triggered the FDA lookup. Pre-fix, FDA-resolved catalog data stayed local — peer devices had to wait for their own background FDA lookup before the name/dose appeared (Alex's 30-min Invega Sustenna lag). The fix emits an EditProductOp every time _saveFdaInfo persists new fields, with operator-curated drug_name still protected from any cross-device overwrite. (2) A-Label-2: the printed fallback label's barcode is now ~25% narrower. Wire format dropped from 40 chars to 30 (RFC 4648 base32 of the UUID instead of dashed-hex). Reclaims margin on the DYMO 450 1×2⅛" stock Alex uses. Previously printed labels stay scannable indefinitely — parser accepts both v1 and legacy forms; no DB migration. (3) Sprint 2 reconciliation refinements: new categorization (match / rounding / over / under / missing-from-Liberty) with 5% rounding threshold to suppress scan-timing noise. Active inventory whose GTIN doesn't appear in the Liberty export is surfaced as its own category, separate from Liberty's enumerated zero-qty rows. (4) Finding 5 cleanup: the legacy SyncRunner.appendOp surface now records lastError on transport failure so the diagnostics screen shows a consistent story regardless of which push path ran. Race-safe ProductDao additions (insertIfMissing + fillNullColumns using SQLite atomic COALESCE updates) close a Codex-caught P1 stale-cache race that could have permanently diverged cross-device catalog state. Three-reviewer audit shipped — see docs/audit-results/20260527_late_night_audit.md. 657 tests passing (+23 vs v0.31.0). Auto-updater rolls v0.31.0 → v0.32.0 on next launch.
